channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Elden Ring Tarnished Edition Nintendo Switch 2 Release Date Announced
item.content:encoded:
The release date for the Elden Ring Tarnished Edition on Nintendo Switch 2 has been announced, with the included content also set to be available on other platforms.

Nintendo’s launch of the Switch 2 last year has significantly increased the number of games playable on the console, bringing a host of titles that the original Switch did not have the performance capacity to run into the fray.

With the likes of Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 among those coming to Switch 2, the release roster has increased further with the reveal of the date Elden Ring lands on the platform.

Elden Ring Tarnished Edition Launches August 28

Posting on social media, it was announced that the Elden Ring Tarnished Edition is launching on Nintendo Switch 2 on August 28, 2026, but the news doesn’t only appeal to Nintendo gamers.

A follow-up tweet confirmed that the additional content included in the Elden Ring Tarnished Edition will also be available for purchase on other platforms, with the DLC releasing on the same date.

The Tarnished Edition includes the base game of Elden Ring and Shadow of the Erdtree, alongside new weapons, armour Torrent skins, and more.

Elden Ring was announced to be coming to Switch 2 earlier this year with the launch of the Tarnished Edition, but no precise release date had been available until now.

The Tarnished Edition will be available for $79.99 on Nintendo Switch 2, and while a specific price for the content on other platforms is yet to be announced, it’s expected to be at around $5.

Are you excited by the launch of Elden Ring on Nintendo Switch 2? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Nintendo, read about the new hardware changes coming to Switch 2, and check out claims of a Nintendo Direct next week.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Nintendo Announces New Switch 2 Hardware With Replaceable Batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo has announced new hardware to align with fresh legislation from the European Union, aimed at reducing the environmental impact, disposal, and recycling of batteries.

As such, Nintendo is making changes that will result in it becoming much easier to replace batteries in the Nintendo Switch 2 console, and potentially Joy-Con controllers.

New EU Legislation Leads to Nintendo Changes

Announced by Nintendo, and spotted by Digital Foundry, current products with a ‘BEE’ model number will receive ‘OSM’ numbers, which include the Nintendo Switch 2 console, and possibly the Joy-Con controllers.

The aim of the legislation is to reduce e-waste and extend the life of battery-powered products, which may otherwise end in the trash due to deteriorating batteries and, crucially in Nintendo’s case, the lack of repairability.

As a result, the OSM model of the Switch 2 will ensure that heat guns or battery solvents are not required to free the battery, and that no custom screws are used to hold the case together.

It’s not confirmed whether the same rules will apply to the Joy-Con controllers, but Nintendo may be required to make adjustments.

Additionally, Nintendo has an obligation to supply replacement batteries for up to five years after products are discontinued, and it opens the door for third-party manufacturers to potentially compete with Nintendo by offering their own replacement batteries.

Digital Foundry also details that it could result in higher-capacity batteries becoming available, extending mobile play time, so it’s a change that is definitely going to help consumers.

What do you think about these Nintendo Switch 2 changes? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Nintendo, read claims about a new Nintendo Direct coming next week, and read about Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4’s launch on Nintendo Switch 2.

item.title: Backyard Baseball Will Have Online Multiplayer
item.content:encoded:
Get ready to head to the backyard with players around the world. Playground Productions has announced that Backyard Baseball will feature online multiplayer for the first time when players head to the diamond on July 9.

The publisher calls the mode “the most requested and most highly anticipated feature from the fan community.”

“We’re incredibly excited to introduce online multiplayer and couch co-op to this new game,” Lindsay Barnett, CEO of Playground Productions, said. “Backyard Baseball has an incredible fan community. With online play and couch co-op, we’re proud to be creating more opportunities for fans to connect and play together.

“Backyard is for kids and kids at heart. We want everyone to play like a kid!”

Announced in January, Backyard Baseball marks the first game in the Backyard Sports franchise since Backyard Sports Baseball 2015. As far as console games go, it’s the first since Backyard Sports: Rookie Rush in 2010.

The game will feature over 30 playable characters, a character creator, classic stadiums with day and night variations, and new game modes such as Backyard Derby. And for those wondering, the game will include a season mode and local co-op, as well.

Backyard Baseball is the first of a planned reboot of the Backyard Sports franchise. Dubbed the “Backyardiverse”, Playground Productions’ goal is to create “a connected world where sports, storytelling, and culture all come together.”

“Backyard Baseball is not simply a throwback; it is the kickoff to a whole new era of Backyard Sports,” they said.

What do you think of online multiplayer coming to Backyard Baseball? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about how you can now punch Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick right in the face. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: All Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan Winter Festival Playlist Events, Challenges & Rewards
item.content:encoded:
A new week in Forza Horizon 6 means a new season of content, and this week marks the start of Winter in the Welcome to Japan Playlist, the first time Winter has appeared in the game.

Festival Playlists in Forza Horizon 6 are spread across four weeks, each featuring a different season with map changes and challenges, before moving on to the next playlist.

For all the Challenges to complete this week and all the rewards available, Insider Gaming has the details you need.

All Welcome to Japan Winter Festival Playlist Daily Challenges & Rewards

As usual, Daily Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 drop on a daily basis, but they remain available until the end of the current season, and potentially even longer.

This means that you don’t have to make sure you log into the game daily to tick off the objectives, and can instead stack them up to create a to-do list to tick off before the end of the season.

The seven Daily Challenges for the Winter season in the Welcome to Japan Festival Playlist and the rewards they provide are outlined in the table below.

All Welcome to Japan Winter Festival Playlist Weekly Challenges & Rewards

Like always, Weekly Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 are themed around a specific car, and this week’s vehicle of focus is the BMW M2 Competition Coupe.

You’ll need to own the car to tick through the challenges, which must be completed in the set order, and the final reward is Five Festival Points and 25,000 Credits.

Each step of the Weekly Challenge in the Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan Autumn Festival Playlist is detailed below.

  1. Own and Drive the 2020 BMW M2 Competition Coupe.
  2. Earn 3 Stars at Speed Traps in the vehicle.
  3. Take a Photo of the vehicle.
  4. Complete 3 laps at the Soni Time Attack in the vehicle.

All Welcome to Japan Winter Festival Playlist Events & Rewards

The table below highlights all other Seasonal events and challenges in the Welcome to Japan Winter Festival Playlist, and all the available rewards.

What do you make of these rewards? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, check out the likely release window for God of War: Laufey, and find out the remake status of Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis.
